creator: thierry@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->feedback


Thanks for your interest, but I don't think that your patch is necessary: 

- in this port, $PREFIX is redefined to include mpich2, and the shared 
libs are registered by ldconfig 

. see the end of a log, between "Compressing manual pages" and 
"Registering installation", e.g. at 
<http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-6-full-logs/mpich2-1.0.5p3,2.log> 
you can see: 
===>   Running ldconfig 
/sbin/ldconfig -m /usr/local/mpich2/lib 

. then, if you type `ldconfig -r | grep mpich2', you should get 
something like: 
search directories: /lib:/usr/lib:/usr/lib/compat:/usr/X11R6/lib:/usr/local/lib:/usr/local/lib/compat/pkg:/usr/local/lib/gcc-4.2.0:/usr/local/lib/graphviz:/usr/local/lib/mplayer/vidix:/usr/local/lib/mysql:/usr/local/lib/pth:/usr/local/lib/kde3:/usr/local/mpich2/lib:/usr/local/lib/pinot:/usr/local/lib/pinot/filters 
1120:-lfmpich.0 => /usr/local/mpich2/lib/libfmpich.so.0 
1121:-lmpich.0 => /usr/local/mpich2/lib/libmpich.so.0 

If the output on your machine does not include /usr/local/mpich2/lib 
could you please report it to me? 

- Could you please report the ouput of 
make -V MAKE_ENV -C /usr/ports/net/mpich2 
before and after applying your patch? 
Notice the values of PREFIX and of LD_RUN_PATH after your patch. 

- scalapack-1.7.5 builds succesfully on the packages cluster; see 
<http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-6-latest-logs/scalapack-1.7.5.log> 
(notice the line with "===>   scalapack-1.7.5 depends on shared 
library: mpich - found"). 

Best regards,
